title: How do you handle conflicts with teammates?
content: I focus on the problem, not the person. I listen to their perspective first, then share mine with concrete examples. If we can't agree, I suggest we prototype both approaches or bring in a third opinion.

title: REST vs GraphQL
content: REST is simpler and works well for CRUD-heavy services with predictable data shapes. GraphQL shines when clients need flexible queries and you want to avoid over-fetching. I choose based on the use case — REST for internal microservices, GraphQL for client-facing APIs with diverse consumers.

title: Describe a challenging bug you solved
content: We had intermittent 502 errors under load. Logs showed nothing. I added distributed tracing and found that a downstream service was hitting its connection pool limit. The fix was switching from short-lived connections to a persistent pool with proper timeout settings. Response errors dropped to zero.

title: Why are you leaving your current job?
content: I've grown a lot in my current role, but I'm looking for new challenges — particularly in larger-scale systems and a team where I can both contribute and learn from senior engineers.

title: How do you stay up to date with technology?
content: I read engineering blogs from companies like Netflix and Uber, follow key developers on GitHub, and build small side projects to try new tools. I also attend local meetups when I can.
